name: Spinach Maria
recipeIngredient: 5 frozen spinach packages, chopped (10 oz each)
4 ounces Monterey jack cheese (for topping), grated
recipeInstructions:
1. Thaw spinach in the refrigerator for 24 hours.
2. Squeeze as much excess water as possible out of the spinach.
3. Heat milk and spices in a 4-quart saucepan over medium heat until just below a boil (190°F). Reduce heat and simmer.
4. Finely chop onion and sauté in 1 tablespoon butter over medium heat for 5-8 minutes.
5. Add the chopped onion to the saucepan.
6. Combine 5 tablespoons melted butter with flour in a small saucepan. This is the first step of making a roux, which will thicken the sauce. Mix until completely blended. Cook on low heat for 3-4 minutes to make the roux.
7. Add the roux to the milk in the saucepan and mix well. Continue cooking until the sauce thickens.
8. Cut Velveeta, cheddar, and jack cheeses into small cubes and add them to the saucepan. Continue mixing until all the cheese is completely melted and blended into the sauce.
9. Be careful not to burn the sauce while the cheese is melting. Remove from heat and allow to cool for 15 minutes.
10. Add drained spinach to the cheese sauce, mix until completely blended.
11. Spoon the mixture into a 11 x 9 x 2-inch (or large) casserole dish.
12. Top with grated Monterey Jack cheese.
13. Bake at 350°F for 12-15 minutes, until hot and bubbly.
